headlines.title: Iran War Live Updates: Reported Seizure of Ships Injects New Uncertainty Over Peace Talks
headlines.summary: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards said they had seized two ships near the Strait of Hormuz. A Trump spokeswoman said the president did not view those actions as violating the cease-fire.

headlines.title: Threat of Evictions Darkens Russia’s Rosy Picture of Occupied Ukraine
headlines.summary: A new law forces Ukrainians in the captured regions to get Russian title deeds or risk losing their homes.

headlines.title: To Iran, Trump Blinked First by Extending the Cease-Fire
headlines.summary: Iran’s leaders believe that they can withstand an enduring standoff longer than President Trump. The strategy could be economically devastating for average Iranians.

headlines.title: Pope Leo Is Skipping Some of Africa’s Biggest Catholic Nations
headlines.summary: As the pontiff arrives in the tiny authoritarian nation of Equatorial Guinea, some Catholics in Africa say they are excited about his visit but are feeling a little left out.

headlines.title: A Year After Terrorist Attack, a Kashmir Town Longs for Tourists
headlines.summary: Pahalgam, with steep ravines, grassy hillsides and pine forests, is a base for Hindu pilgrimages that business operators hope will revive visitors.

headlines.title: ‘Blockade and threats’: Iran blames US siege of ports for stalled talks
South American migrants deported to DRC say facing pressure to return home
Top ministers quit after Peru’s president postpones F-16 fighter jet deal
Prediction market Kalshi docks three US candidates for betting on own races
Turkiye making efforts to revive Russia-Ukraine talks, says Erdogan
Chemical leak at a W.Va. plant kills 2 people, sends 30 more to hospitals, officials say
headlines.summary: The leak occurred at the Catalyst Refiners plant, a silver recovery business. An emergency management official says workers were preparing to shut down at least part of the facility when the leak occurred, causing a chemical gas reaction.

headlines.title: Pentagon says Navy secretary is leaving, the latest departure of a top defense leader
headlines.summary: Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said John Phelan, the Navy's top civilian official, was "departing the administration, effective immediately." Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become acting secretary of the Navy.

headlines.title: Tesla's making money. But it's planning to spend an awful lot more
headlines.summary: Tesla's profits were up from this time last year. But the company warned investors to prepare for expensive investments in next-generation technology like humanoid robots and AI.

headlines.title: The tariff refund process has begun for businesses. What about customers?
headlines.summary: While shipping companies are pledging refunds for customers who directly paid tariff fees, the situation is much trickier for retailers.

headlines.title: Inside Linda McMahon's effort to dismantle the Department of Education
headlines.summary: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.

headlines.title: Mutual gains via Five-Year Plan welcomed
headlines.summary: China and the US can create a better future for relations, Chinese Ambassador to the United States Xie Feng said on Monday, calling on the nations to seize the opportunities presented by China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-30).

headlines.title: Shared future envisioned in outline
headlines.summary: Fifty measures were unveiled on Wednesday at the third High-Level Conference of the Forum on Global Action for Shared Development, underscoring China's commitment to advancing global development.

headlines.title: UK inflation rises sharply as impact of Iran war felt
headlines.summary: The Iran war and resulting hike in the price of oil has caused a sharp increase in the United Kingdom's inflation rate, pushing it up from 3 percent in February to 3.3 percent in the year to March.

headlines.title: Cambodian PM meets with Chinese foreign minister, defense minister
headlines.summary: PHNOM PENH - Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet met with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi and Defense Minister Dong Jun on Wednesday here in Phnom Penh, capital of Cambodia, vowing to further deepen bilateral ties and cooperation.

headlines.title: Europe's defense vulnerable to US cloud providers
headlines.summary: Most European countries rely on United States-based cloud services for national defense, creating the strategic risk of a "kill switch" that could disable systems, a new analysis concludes.

headlines.title: Shutdowns by ethnic groups disrupt normal life in most parts of Manipur
headlines.summary: Shutdowns by ethnic groups in Manipur disrupt daily life amid ongoing protests over recent violence and government inaction.

headlines.title: On Pahalgam attack anniversary, Army says ‘response is assured’ against acts of terror
headlines.summary: On Pahalgam attack anniversary, Indian Army vows assured response to terrorism, emphasizing ongoing Operation Sindoor and high military readiness.

headlines.title: India will never bow before forces spreading hatred, fear: Rahul on Pahalgam attack anniversary
headlines.summary: Congress emphasizes India's unity against terrorism on the Pahalgam attack anniversary, honoring victims and criticizing government foreign policy failures.

headlines.title: Congress candidate moves SC against telecast of PM’s address
headlines.summary: Congress candidate T.N. Prathapan petitions Supreme Court against PM Modi's partisan address, alleging misuse of state media during elections.

headlines.title: Pahalgam terror attack: Govt appoints Karambir Singh Nalwa as Special Public Prosecutor to lead trial
headlines.summary: Government appoints Karambir Singh Nalwa as Special Public Prosecutor for trial in Pahalgam terror attack case involving Lashkar-e-Taiba.
